At day 20 of cycling, nitrate accumulation (80ppm) is driving pH decline—ammonia/nitrite both zero is good, but the byproduct acid is outpacing the tank's buffering capacity. Not yet dangerous (6.49 is still habitable), but trajectory matters. If pH continues dropping 0.08/day, we'll hit 6.0 in ~6 days, which is the hard floor.
recommended_actions: Monitor pH every 12 hours for next 48h to confirm acceleration or stabilization.
Consider partial water change (25-30%) if pH drops below 6.2 or continues declining at current rate.
Retest ammonia/nitrite/nitrate tomorrow to confirm cycling stage and buffer consumption rate.
Do not introduce livestock until pH stabilizes above 6.5 or we've actively raised it via water change.
suggested_actuator_actions: No immediate action required. Hold all adjustments pending 48h pH trend confirmation.
If pH reaches 6.2, prepare for water change (25-30% RO/dechlorinated) to dilute accumulated acids and replenish buffering capacity.

tds.note: Within target 150–250ppm. Rising (+7ppm/24hr) tracks expected nitrate accumulation; no concern yet.

reasoning: pH sits at 6.48—just outside safe range and trending downward (−0.07 over 24h). Latest ammonia/nitrite at zero and nitrate at 80ppm confirms active cycling; acid accumulation from bioload is expected. Decline is gradual, not sharp—buffer capacity intact—but trajectory warrants close watch. Temp and TDS are solid.
recommended_actions: Monitor pH drop over next 48 hours. If pH drops below 6.2 or shows acceleration, flag for buffer intervention.
Continue daily testing (ammonia, nitrite, nitrate, pH). Current cycle state is healthy—ride it out.
Plan water change window around day 22–23 when nitrate peaks (expect 100–120ppm). Small 20–25% change will reset pH and TDS simultaneously.
Observe shrimp behavior. No acclimation stress expected yet, but note any lethargy or odd molting patterns.
suggested_actuator_actions: No immediate action. Temperature stability is excellent—heater/chiller working well.
Hold water change until day 22–23 unless pH drops to 6.0 or below (then intervene immediately).

temperature.note: Stable, within range. Slight 24h decline is normal.

ph.note: Just below target floor (6.5). Trending down 0.09/24h. Monitor closely for acceleration.

tds.note: Mid-range, stable. Slight rise expected as tank matures and nitrate accumulates.

reasoning: pH has dipped 0.09 below target in 24h, consistent with acid accumulation as the cycle matures. Nitrate at 80ppm on day 20 is expected; buffering capacity appears intact but declining trend warrants vigilance. This is not yet critical—we're in amber territory, not red.
recommended_actions: Monitor pH daily through day 22 to establish trend slope. Flag if next reading <6.3 or if 24h decline exceeds 0.15.
Hold on water changes for now—small daily pH shifts are normal during cycling. Premature intervention risks destabilizing alkalinity.
Continue ammonia/nitrite monitoring; if either returns, prioritize that over pH.
suggested_actuator_actions: No actuator action at this time. pH decline is modest and predictable for day 20 of a cycle. Observe before intervening.
